title: Future of Marketing Briefing: OpenAI is working with Skai to bring retail and commerce advertisers into ChatGPT
description: Like the Criteo deal before it, the idea is to give advertisers a route into ChatGPT inventory through infrastructure they already use.

title: How Content Marketers Misuse GenAI
description:
The marketers who benefit most from generative AI will likely not produce more content but will instead exercise discretion.
The post How Content Marketers Misuse GenAI appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: As Target, Walmart and Aldi embrace naturally-colored food, brands rush to meet demand
description: Major retailers like Walmart and Target are removing artificial food dyes from their aisles, especially in products like cereals and frozen treats. This has been a boon for startups already carrying naturally-colored products in their respective categories.

title: Dick’s Sporting Goods brings sales growth back to Foot Locker in Q1
description:
Dick’s Sporting Goods said its newly acquired Foot Locker business returned to comparable sales growth and profitability in Q1 — a sign that the retailer’s turnaround efforts are starting to take hold. For its fiscal quarter ended May 2, Dick’s reported a 6.0% increase in comparable sales for its namesake banner. During the same period, […]
The post Dick’s Sporting Goods brings sales growth back to Foot Locker in Q1 appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Ecommerce Trends: Which retailers could lower prices after tariff refunds
description:
Throughout 2025 and 2026, retailers have adjusted projections, supply chain strategies and pricing as tariffs fluctuated — and now, as some of the biggest names in online retail pursue tariff refunds, some merchants are considering lower prices as a next step. What is already clear is that tariff refunds are beginning to flow in the […]
The post Ecommerce Trends: Which retailers could lower prices after tariff refunds appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: How Chipotle’s rewards relaunch extends reach of its marketing flywheel
description: Curt Garner, chief strategy and technology officer at the chain, explains how a “Summer of Extras” effort builds on and gamifies its rewards program.

title: OpenAI turns on cost-per-action ads inside ChatGPT
description: Cost-per-action (CPA) is the first real sign that the platform is now embracing performance advertising.

title: AI is not the skill email marketers need most
description:
The most valuable email marketers know how to apply AI across automation, personalization, analytics, testing, and customer journeys.
The post AI is not the skill email marketers need most appeared first on MarTech.

title: AI agents are exposing martech’s weak point
description:
A new API report card reveals major gaps in the systems marketers depend on for automation and AI workflows.
The post AI agents are exposing martech’s weak point appeared first on MarTech.

title: 3 things you must know to get AI-native advertising right
description:
Conversational AI is reshaping advertising through real-time recommendations, dynamic creative, and autonomous optimization.
The post 3 things you must know to get AI-native advertising right appeared first on MarTech.

title: Marketplace Briefing: Why Amazon is selling its AI-powered shopping tools to other retailers
description: Amazon is starting to sell the technology behind its AI shopping assistant to other retailers, as the e-commerce giant looks to become a bigger player in the fast-growing market for AI-powered shopping tools.

title: How GenAI Platforms Produce Answers
description:
A useful first step toward genAI visibility is understanding the answer-creation process. 
The post How GenAI Platforms Produce Answers appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: How to optimize for AI overviews (AIOs): A complete 2026 playbook
description: Google AI Overviews appear in Google Search results for a growing share of queries, and if your content isn’t structured to earn a citation, you’re losing visibility to competitors who’ve already adapted. Unfortunately, the challenge isn’t awareness. Most SEO leaders know AI Overviews exist. The challenge is execution: translating Google’s deliberately vague guidance into repeatable content workflows, measuring whether your AI website optimizations are actually earning citations, and proving business impact when traditional metrics like rank position and CTR no longer tell the full story. This playbook closes that gap.

title: We Debunk 5 Misconceptions About Email Marketing
description: No, email is not perfect, but many of its challenges are solvable if you go about it the right way. Here's how.

title: #213: AI Answers - What AI Should Never Do, Enterprise Scaling, Governing AI & Navigating IT Roadblocks
description:
Leadership wants AI everywhere. IT security can't keep up. Marketing is racing ahead while legal and finance dig in. And every week brings another story of an AI agent doing something nobody told it to do. Paul Roetzer and Cathy McPhillips answer 15 listener questions on how to actually move organizations forward and where the real opportunities lie for individuals, SMBs, and enterprises right now.

00:00:00 — Intro

00:07:01 — How do you move a company out of AI policy paralysis?

00:08:54 — How should regulated, hands-on teams introduce AI?

00:12:30 — When companies are stuck, what tends to get them moving?

00:15:00 — Should IT security evolve to adopt AI quicker or should businesses slow down?

00:17:43 — What changes an AI-skeptic employee's mind?

00:21:18 — How should early-career professionals prioritize what to learn?

00:23:29 — Is there a point where you should stop learning and start building?

00:27:29 — Where do companies get stuck scaling AI across departments?

00:29:4
